items.front: What weather awareness is required when a controller comes on duty?
items.back: Become familiar with pertinent weather and stay aware of current and forecast weather needed to perform ATC duties.

items.front: Which PIREPs receive special emphasis for solicitation and dissemination?
items.back: Urgent (UUA) and Routine (UA) PIREPs, with particular attention to significant weather and flight-safety conditions.

items.front: If tower visibility and surface visibility differ below 4 miles, which value is used for aircraft operations?
items.back: Use the lower of the two observations.

items.front: How should weather or chaff areas be defined for potentially affected aircraft?
items.back: By clock azimuth and distance from the aircraft, and or by the width and area of coverage using fixes or distance and direction from fixes.

items.front: When may a terminal controller transmit specific ceiling or visibility values?
items.back: Only if the controller is an authorized observer, got the value from the official observer, received a verified weather report, or obtained it from an FAA-approved automated surface weather system.

items.front: How far from the sector or area of jurisdiction must controllers advise pilots about hazardous inflight weather?
items.back: Within 150 nautical miles, if the weather may affect operations.
